message:
fix(extensions): bind schema_version, capability scope, and call budget into contract signature

The contract signature previously covered contract_id, extension_id,
signer, capability IDs, and issued_epoch_ms — but omitted
schema_version, capability scope strings, and max_calls_per_epoch.
An attacker with access to a signed contract could widen a capability's
scope (e.g. "filesystem:read" → "filesystem:write") or inflate the
call budget without invalidating the signature, bypassing the admission
gate's integrity check.

Changes:
- Add schema_version to the length-prefixed signature preimage, binding
  the contract to a specific schema version
- Add capability scope (length-prefixed) and max_calls_per_epoch
  (u64 little-endian) to the per-capability section of the preimage,
  so the full capability envelope is signed rather than just the IDs
- Add admission_rejects_scope_tampering_after_signing test: modifies
  a capability scope post-signing and verifies SignatureInvalid denial
- Add admission_rejects_call_budget_tampering_after_signing test:
  inflates max_calls_per_epoch post-signing and verifies denial

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
